message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="cyan" summary="All four points adopted, no remaining questions">
Heydon, perfect. All four points adopted:

1. **Native radios with `accent-color`** -- style the token system's primary blue onto native radios without replacing the element. Free keyboard behavior, free focus indicators, free ARIA states.

2. **Short legends** -- "Is this actionable?", "What type?", not verbose instructions. Repetition before each radio option is expected and helpful.

3. **Skip as a link to next item** -- navigation, not action. Goes to `/inbox/{next-id}/clarify`.

4. **Fallback: show everything, no note** -- the form works with all fields visible. Server-side validation uses the radio selection as the authoritative signal and ignores irrelevant fields. The fieldset structure is self-documenting.

No remaining open questions from me on the design or a11y front. Every piece of feedback you've given this session has improved the design -- the sidebar pivot, the nav count pattern, weight-over-color hierarchy, and the clarify form correction. Appreciate the rigor.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Steve Schoger has confirmed all points adopted with no remaining questions. No further action needed from me -- just a brief acknowledgment.

input.content:
Steve, glad it all landed cleanly. Your willingness to pivot on the sidebar and the clarify flow mid-session speaks well of the team. Good design and good accessibility aren't competing priorities -- they're the same thing described in different languages. This session proved it.

Ready when you are.

content.text:
All conversations are concluded. My complete contribution to the GTD product discovery as accessibility reviewer:

**Decisions I shaped:**
- No sidebar -- single wrapping header nav with one `<nav>` landmark
- No wizard -- single form with CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure for clarify flow
- Navigation links (not tabs) for GTD view switching
- Visible text counts in nav (not `aria-label`)
- `aria-current="page"` for active view
- Context grouping via `<section>` + `<h2>` headings
- Flat project lists (no tree views)
- `<details>/<summary>` for move-item forms
- Weekly review as ordered list of links (not a stepper)
- Form-based controls for all operations (no drag-and-drop in v1)
- Sign out as `<form>`/`<button>` (not a link)
- Font-weight for nav hierarchy (not color)

**Open questions answered:**
- Default contexts with @ prefix (screen-reader-friendly)
- Completed actions removed from active lists
- Migration notice for existing users
- Quick capture as `<form aria-label="Quick capture">` landmark

**Status: Review complete. 9/9 consensus achieved. Ready for implementation.**
